target.name: Nominal
target.founder: Cameron McCord, Bryce Strauss, Jason Hoch

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.0B - $3.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Nominal is a Series B-2 stage AI & Software company currently valued at $1.0B, having raised $155M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 same-sector, similar-valuation-tier companies spanning Series A through IPO stages, with a broader comparable universe of 9 total private firms and 1 public reference point (Palantir, ticker PLTR).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The five selected peers—Ayar Labs ($3.8B, Series E), Together AI ($3.3B, Series A), Black Forest Labs ($3.2B, Series B), Lambda ($2.5B, Series E), and Field AI ($2.0B, IPO)—yield a median valuation of $3.2B, with a range spanning $2.0B to $3.8B. This method directly reflects transaction activity and investor pricing within the target's sector and valuation tier.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.8B to $3.7B, centered on $3.2B. The current $1.0B valuation sits materially below peer medians, suggesting either a discount reflecting stage, traction, or governance factors relative to comparables, or potential upside to peer-level pricing upon next funding event. Key uncertainties include revenue run-rate, unit economics, and burn profile relative to peers—data not present in this analysis. The peer set's stage heterogeneity (ranging from Series A to IPO) introduces some methodological noise in benchmarking a Series B-2 company.
